item.title: Trump says U.S. deal with Iran "is now complete"
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are expected to meet for a signing ceremony on Friday, June 19, in Switzerland, Pakistan's prime minister said.

item.title: Iran and U.S. reach peace deal, Trump and Pakistani prime minister say
item.description: Pakistan's prime minister said Sunday the U.S. and Iran had reached a deal that includes "the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon."

item.title: 11 skydivers and pilot killed in plane crash near Kansas City
item.description: The Missouri State Highway Patrol said the fatal crash occurred near the Butler Memorial Airport, about 60 miles south of Kansas City.

item.title: Singer Oliver Tree among 6 killed in helicopter collision over Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's Military Fire Department said one of the helicopters crashed in the parking lot of a car dealership, where several electric vehicles were parked, igniting a fire.

item.title: Mitch McConnell admitted to the hospital, spokesperson says
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell was admitted to the hospital Sunday morning, a spokesperson for the Republican confirmed to CBS News.

item.title: Hegseth: "Manufactured story" that U.S. faces munitions stockpile shortage
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before the Senate Armed Service Committee earlier this year that replenishing the stockpile could take "months and years."

item.title: Transcript: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 14, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 14, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Gary Cohn on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 14, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Gary Cohn, IBM vice chairman and director of the National Economic Council during President Trump's first term, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 14, 2026.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 14, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, Defense Secretary and Sen. Mark Warner join Margaret Brennan.

item.title: Transcript: Sen. Mark Kelly on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 14, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Sen. Mark Kelly, Democrat of Arizona, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 14, 2026.

item.title: Sen. Mark Warner says he hopes Senate can confirm Trump's DNI pick "this week"
item.description: The Senate has been at an impasse over the president's controversial pick to serve as acting intelligence chief that resulted in the expiration of a key spy authority.

item.title: Transcript: Sen. Mark Warner on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 14, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Sen. Mark Warner, Demcorat of Virginia, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 14, 2026.

item.title: Organic formula recalled after 3 babies diagnosed with infant botulism
item.description: The affected formula was sold at Target and at Nara.com, according to the Food and Drug Administration.

item.title: Trump speaks with Putin and Zelenskyy as war in Ukraine rages on
item.description: President Trump told Russia's Putin that the war in Ukraine needed to end. Ukraine's Zelenskyy said he and Mr. Trump would speak more at the upcoming G7 summit.

item.title: Face the Nation: Warner, Kelly, Cohn
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? Sens. Mark Kelly, Mark Warner and IBM vice chairman Gary Cohn join.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 14)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Gary Cohn says prices are "not going to fall like a rock overnight" if Iran deal is signed
item.description: Gary Cohn, IBM vice chairman and the head of the National Economic Council in President Trump's first term, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that prices are "not going to fall like a rock overnight" if a truce between the U.S. and Iran is signed because "we're going to have to see exactly what happens with the Strait" of Hormuz.

item.title: 6/14: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Jane Pauley. Featured: The lack of obesity in Japan; Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance; former child star Bill Mumy; the past and future of the Houston Astrodome; a time capsule for America's 500th birthday; and soccer art made with gum wrappers.

item.title: Trump endorses Rep. Mike Collins in Georgia Senate runoff
item.description: President Trump endorsed Rep. Mike Collins in the Republican Senate runoff in Georgia, wading into the race days ahead of the contest that will decide who takes on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff.

item.title: Sen. Mark Kelly says "of course we have a munitions issue" despite Hegseth denial
item.description: Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly, who has sparred with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth after Hegseth's testimony before the Senate Armed Services Committee, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "of course we have a munitions issue" amid the Iran war, adding that it's "widely understood that when you attack over 10,000 targets from the air with cruise missiles and ballistic missiles and bombs from airplanes, you are using a lot of munitions, and we do not have an endless supply of these things."

item.title: 6/14: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as Qatari mediators travel to Tehran to finalize the truce in the U.S.-Iran war, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth joins. Plus, Democratic Sens. Mark Warner and Mark Kelly join.

item.title: Vance: Trump "very supportive" as vice president moves closer to 2028 decision
item.description: Vice President JD Vance tells "CBS Sunday Morning" that he and his wife, Usha, will make a decision whether to enter the 2028 presidential race following the 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: JD and Usha Vance on faith and family
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and his wife, Usha, are expecting their fourth child. They talk about family; his book, "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith"; and his future.

item.title: "Do not open until July 4, 2276": What will America's Time Capsule reveal?
item.description: To mark America's 250th, a time capsule will be buried in Philadelphia on July 4, not to be opened until America's quincentennial. What objects made the cut to be preserved for another 250 years?

item.title: White House hosting UFC fight tonight on Trump's 80th birthday
item.description: The UFC is hosting a fight series on the White House South Lawn Sunday night.

item.title: Body found outside Tijuana stadium where Iran team is training for World Cup
item.description: Mexican authorities are investigating how a corpse ended up outside a stadium in the border city of Tijuana, where Iran's national team has been training for the World Cup.

item.title: How Boston Dynamics upgraded the Atlas robot
item.description: Atlas, a humanoid robot made by robotics company Boston Dynamics, was upgraded from a version 60 Minutes saw in 2021 with joints that can fully rotate and hands that can grip a variety of objects.

item.title: How Boston Dynamics upgraded the Atlas robot
item.description: Atlas, a humanoid robot made by robotics company Boston Dynamics, was upgraded from a version 60 Minutes saw in 2021 with joints that can fully rotate and hands that can grip a variety of objects.

item.title: Lamine Yamal's extended 60 Minutes interview in Spanish
item.description: In 2025, Spanish soccer star Lamine Yamal sat down with 60 Minutes correspondent Jon Wertheim to talk about his love for soccer, the World Cup, and the pressure of playing on the world stage.

item.title: Lamine Yamal's extended 60 Minutes interview in Spanish
item.description: In 2025, Spanish soccer star Lamine Yamal sat down with 60 Minutes correspondent Jon Wertheim to talk about his love for soccer, the World Cup, and the pressure of playing on the world stage.
